---
title: "XML Pipeline - Code Availability"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Packages

```{r}
#| echo: false
library(xml2)
library(stringr)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)
```

## Configuration

```{r}
# Folder containing GROBID-processed TEI XML files (.tei.xml or .xml)
# Run GROBID on your PDF folder to generate these files.
# GROBID preserves document structure and extracts hyperlinks from ref attributes.
xml_folder <- "C:/Systematic Review/Systematic_Review_Automation/Article Records 1/xml"

# Metadata spreadsheet (same as before)
metadata_file <- "C:/Systematic Review/Systematic_Review_Automation/cleaned_articles_2023_2024_export.xls"

# Output RDS file loaded by RUN_ME.qmd
output_file <- "test-data.rds"

# Rows from all_articles to include in this batch
batch_rows <- 801:1200
```

## Parsing TEI XML files

```{r}
#| echo: false

# Strip invisible Unicode characters that occasionally appear in GROBID output
clean_url <- function(url) {
  str_remove_all(url, "[​‌‍­﻿]")
}

# Parse a single TEI XML file produced by GROBID and return a named list.
# Prioritises <ref type="url" target="..."> for URL extraction — these come
# directly from the PDF's hyperlink layer and are cleaner than regex on text.
parse_tei_xml <- function(xml_path) {
  tryCatch({
    doc  <- read_xml(xml_path)
    ns   <- c(tei = "http://www.tei-c.org/ns/1.0")

    # --- Title ---------------------------------------------------------------
    title_node <- xml_find_first(
      doc, "//tei:titleStmt/tei:title[@level='a'][@type='main']", ns)
    if (is.na(title_node))
      title_node <- xml_find_first(doc, "//tei:titleStmt/tei:title", ns)
    title <- if (!is.na(title_node)) trimws(xml_text(title_node)) else NA_character_

    # --- Abstract ------------------------------------------------------------
    abs_nodes <- xml_find_all(doc, "//tei:profileDesc//tei:abstract//tei:p", ns)
    abstract  <- paste(xml_text(abs_nodes), collapse = " ")
    if (nchar(trimws(abstract)) == 0) abstract <- NA_character_

    # --- Body text -----------------------------------------------------------
    # Collect paragraphs and section headings from body; include back matter
    # (data availability, acknowledgements) because that is where code links live.
    body_nodes <- xml_find_all(
      doc, "//tei:body//*[self::tei:p or self::tei:head][not(ancestor::tei:figure[@type='table'])][not(ancestor::tei:formula)]", ns)
    back_nodes <- xml_find_all(
      doc, "//tei:back//*[self::tei:p or self::tei:head][not(ancestor::tei:listBibl)][not(ancestor::tei:figure[@type='table'])][not(ancestor::tei:formula)]", ns)

    full_text <- paste(
      c(xml_text(body_nodes), xml_text(back_nodes)),
      collapse = " "
    )
    full_text <- gsub("\n", " ", full_text)

    # --- URLs ----------------------------------------------------------------
    # Primary source: target attribute on <ref type="url"> elements.
    # Also capture the parent paragraph as context for each URL.
    url_refs <- xml_find_all(doc, "//*[@type='url']", ns)
    url_data <- map_dfr(url_refs, function(ref) {
      target <- clean_url(xml_attr(ref, "target"))
      if (is.na(target) || !nzchar(target)) return(NULL)
      target  <- str_remove(target, "[.,;:!?]+$")
      context <- trimws(xml_text(xml_parent(ref)))
      tibble(url = target, context = context)
    })

    urls_structured <- if (nrow(url_data) > 0) unique(url_data$url) else character(0)

    # Secondary source: regex over plain text (catches any URL not wrapped in
    # a ref element, e.g. in a footnote or table cell).
    # First, replace each <ref type="url"> element's (potentially space-broken)
    # text content with the clean target URL so the regex sees the correct form.
    full_text_for_regex <- full_text
    for (ref in url_refs) {
      broken <- xml_text(ref)
      tgt    <- clean_url(xml_attr(ref, "target"))
      if (!is.na(tgt) && nzchar(tgt) && nzchar(broken))
        full_text_for_regex <- str_replace_all(full_text_for_regex, fixed(broken), tgt)
    }
    all_text  <- paste(c(abstract, full_text_for_regex), collapse = " ")
    urls_text <- unlist(str_extract_all(all_text, "https?://[^\\s<>\"'\\)\\]]+"))
    urls_text <- str_remove(urls_text, "[.,;:!?]+$")
    urls_text <- sapply(urls_text, clean_url, USE.NAMES = FALSE)

    all_urls <- unique(c(urls_structured, urls_text))
    all_urls <- all_urls[nzchar(all_urls)]

    # --- DOI -----------------------------------------------------------------
    doi_node <- xml_find_first(doc, "//tei:idno[@type='DOI']", ns)
    doi      <- if (!is.na(doi_node)) trimws(xml_text(doi_node)) else NA_character_

    list(title = title, abstract = abstract, full_text = full_text,
         urls = all_urls, url_data = url_data, doi_xml = doi)

  }, error = function(e) {
    warning("Failed to parse: ", basename(xml_path), " — ", e$message)
    list(title = NA_character_, abstract = NA_character_, full_text = NA_character_,
         urls = character(0),
         url_data = tibble(url = character(0), context = character(0)),
         doi_xml = NA_character_)
  })
}

# Discover all XML files (GROBID emits .tei.xml; plain .xml also accepted)
xml_paths <- list.files(xml_folder,
                        pattern = "\\.(tei\\.xml|xml)$",
                        recursive = TRUE,
                        full.names = TRUE)

message("Found ", length(xml_paths), " XML files")

# Build a dataframe row per file
df_fulltext <- map_dfr(xml_paths, function(xml_file) {
  result <- parse_tei_xml(xml_file)

  # doi_no_slash: prefer DOI extracted from XML; fall back to filename convention
  # (same as the PDF pipeline: filename is DOI with "/" replaced by nothing)
  doi_no_slash <- if (!is.na(result$doi_xml)) {
    gsub("/", "", result$doi_xml)
  } else {
    basename(xml_file) %>% str_replace("\\.(tei\\.xml|xml)$", "")
  }

  tibble(
    doi_no_slash = doi_no_slash,
    title_xml    = result$title,
    abstract_xml = result$abstract,
    full_text    = result$full_text,
    urls         = list(result$urls),
    url_data     = list(result$url_data),
    doi_xml      = result$doi_xml
  )
})

message("Parsed ", nrow(df_fulltext), " XML files successfully")
```

## Linking metadata to full text

```{r}
meta_data <- read_excel(metadata_file)

# Normalise DOI for joining
meta_data$doi_no_slash <- gsub("/", "", meta_data$DOI)

full_data <- meta_data %>%
  right_join(df_fulltext, by = "doi_no_slash") %>%
  mutate(
    # Prefer spreadsheet title/abstract; fall back to GROBID-extracted values
    title    = coalesce(`Article Title`, title_xml),
    abstract = coalesce(Abstract, abstract_xml),
    DOI      = coalesce(DOI, doi_xml),
    `DOI Link` = paste0("https://doi.org/", DOI)
  ) %>%
  select(-title_xml, -abstract_xml, -doi_xml)

# Clean up newlines left in any text column
full_data$full_text <- gsub("\n", " ", full_data$full_text)
```

## Keyword definitions

```{r}
# Exact single-word keywords (case-insensitive)
key_phrases_exact <- c("code", "script", "osf", "github", "gitlab",
                       "zenodo", "figshare", "supplement", "replicate",
                       "reproduce", "appendix")

# Gapped phrase pairs: both words must appear within max_gap words of each other
key_phrases_gapped <- list(
  c("code", "shared")
)
max_gap <- 3
```

## Extracting keyword context

```{r}
extract_keyword_context <- function(text, key_phrases_exact, key_phrases_gapped, max_gap) {
  if (is.na(text) || nchar(trimws(text)) == 0)
    return(tibble(keyword = character(0), context = character(0)))

  sentences <- unlist(strsplit(text, "(?<=[.!?])\\s+", perl = TRUE))

  # Returns one row per unique sentence that matches pattern, labelled with kw_label
  contexts_for <- function(kw_label, pattern) {
    idx <- unique(which(str_detect(sentences, pattern)))
    if (length(idx) == 0) return(tibble(keyword = character(0), context = character(0)))
    map_dfr(idx, function(i) {
      tibble(
        keyword = kw_label,
        context = paste(na.omit(c(
          if (i > 1) sentences[i - 1] else NA_character_,
          sentences[i],
          if (i < length(sentences)) sentences[i + 1] else NA_character_
        )), collapse = " ")
      )
    })
  }

  # Prefix match (\bkeyword) catches all case variants and suffixes:
  # e.g. "supplement" also matches Supplement, supplementary, supplemental
  exact_results <- map_dfr(key_phrases_exact, function(kw) {
    contexts_for(kw, regex(paste0("\\b", kw), ignore_case = TRUE))
  })

  gapped_results <- map_dfr(key_phrases_gapped, function(pair) {
    pattern <- regex(
      paste0("\\b", pair[1], "(?:\\s+[\\w,\\.\\!\\?]+){0,", max_gap, "}\\s+\\b", pair[2]),
      ignore_case = TRUE
    )
    contexts_for(paste(pair, collapse = " "), pattern)
  })

  bind_rows(exact_results, gapped_results)
}

full_data <- full_data %>%
  mutate(keyword_data = map(full_text, ~extract_keyword_context(
    .x, key_phrases_exact, key_phrases_gapped, max_gap
  )))

message("Keyword extraction complete")
```

## Save batch for evaluation

```{r}
# Only run this block once to initialise all_articles.rds:
# full_data_init <- full_data %>%
#   mutate(included = FALSE, excluded = FALSE, second_reviewer = FALSE, note = "")
# saveRDS(full_data_init, file = "all_articles.rds")

all_articles <- readRDS(
  "C:/Systematic Review/Systematic_Review_Automation/all_articles.rds")

# Overwrite the rows that have freshly extracted XML data
all_articles <- all_articles %>%
  rows_update(
    full_data %>% select(doi_no_slash, full_text, urls, keyword_data),
    by = "doi_no_slash"
  )

# Take the batch subset and save for the reviewer
df_batch <- all_articles[batch_rows, ]
saveRDS(df_batch, file = output_file)
message("Saved ", nrow(df_batch), " articles to: ", output_file)
```

\[... use RUN_ME.qmd to record decisions before continuing below ...\]

## Second reviewer articles

```{r}
decisions <- readRDS(
  "C:/Systematic Review/Systematic_Review_Automation/101-200_Anouk-Robbie.rds")

evaluate_again <- decisions %>%
  filter(second_reviewer == TRUE)

saveRDS(evaluate_again, file = "second_review.rds")
```

## Merge decisions back into main file

```{r}
all_articles <- readRDS(
  "C:/Systematic Review/Systematic_Review_Automation/all_articles.rds")

decisions <- readRDS(
  "C:/Systematic Review/Systematic_Review_Automation/101-200_Anouk-Robbie.rds")

all_articles <- all_articles %>%
  rows_update(decisions, by = "doi_no_slash")

saveRDS(all_articles, file = "all_articles.rds")
```
